title: University or Trade School: Which is Best for You?
passage:
Choosing Your Path: University or Vocational School?

Paragraph A: After finishing high school, young people must make an important choice for their future. They can choose to go to a university, which is called higher education. Or, they can go to a vocational school, which is also called professional job training. Both paths help students get good jobs, but they are very different in many ways.

Paragraph B: Universities focus on academic learning. At university, students spend three or four years studying books, listening to lectures, and writing long essays. They learn about general subjects like history, science, language, or business. After they pass all their exams, they get a university degree. This path is very important for people who want to become doctors, school teachers, or office managers. However, university can be expensive, and students do not start working or earning money for several years.

Paragraph C: Vocational schools focus on practical skills. Students do not spend all day reading books. Instead, they practice doing a specific job with their hands. They work in classrooms that look like real kitchens, car garages, or construction sites. These courses are much shorter, usually lasting only one or two years. Students learn skills to become chefs, car mechanics, plumbers, or builders. This path is cheaper than university, and students can start working and earning money very quickly.

Paragraph D: How do you choose the right path? It depends on what you like. If you love reading, doing research, and want an office job, university is a great choice. If you prefer active work, enjoy fixing things, and want to finish school fast, vocational training is the best option for you.

questions.explanation: Paragraph A states: 'Or, they can go to a vocational school, which is also called professional job training.'

questions.explanation: Paragraph C states vocational courses are 'much shorter, usually lasting only one or two years' compared to university courses which take 'three or four years' (Paragraph B).

vocabulary.meaning: related to subjects study in school or university, focusing on books rather than practical skills.
providing skills and education that prepare you for a specific trade or job.
relating to experience, hands-on activity, or real-world use rather than theory.
an official qualification given to a student who has completed a course of study at a university.

tactics:
Hướng dẫn làm bài (A1-A2)

1. Kỹ thuật Scanning (Tìm từ khóa nhanh): Ở trình độ A1-A2, các câu hỏi sử dụng từ ngữ gần như giống hệt trong bài đọc. Hãy khoanh tròn các từ khóa như tên nghề nghiệp (doctors, chefs), số năm (three or four, one or two) để tìm vị trí thông tin nhanh nhất.

2. Tránh bẫy True/False/Not Given: Nếu một thông tin hoàn toàn không được nhắc đến trong bài (ví dụ: sở thích của phần lớn học sinh), hãy chọn ngay NOT GIVEN chứ không tự suy luận theo ý kiến cá nhân.

3. Điền từ chính xác: Với dạng bài điền từ, hãy chép chính xác từ có sẵn trong bài đọc, không thay đổi dạng từ (ví dụ: giữ nguyên số ít/số nhiều).
